description_long: This is a classic yellow biplane with a streamlined fuselage and two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically. The aircraft features a single propeller mounted at the nose, with a radial engine visible behind it. The cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing, and appears to be enclosed with a clear canopy. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both with rounded edges. The landing gear is fixed, with two main wheels under the lower wing and a small tailwheel at the rear. The overall design is reminiscent of vintage aerobatic or sport aircraft, with visible support struts and tension wires between the wings.
description: A vintage yellow biplane with a single propeller, enclosed cockpit, and fixed landing gear. It features two stacked wings and a classic tail design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the yellow fuselage, stacked wings, and the propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin wings, tail assembly, and landing gear alignment.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the cockpit, propeller, and wing struts.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the radial engine, propeller, and the symmetrical biplane wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing with a clear canopy.
